---
title: "Forecasts"
subtitle: "Model predictions vs realized data through time"
---

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
forecasts = {
  try {
    return await FileAttachment("_data/rolling_forecasts.json").json();
  } catch(e) {
    return [];
  }
}

actuals = {
  try {
    return await FileAttachment("_data/actuals_timeseries.json").json();
  } catch(e) {
    return [];
  }
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  if (forecasts.length === 0) {
    return html`
      <div class="chart-container" style="text-align:center; padding:3rem;">
        <p style="font-size:1.2rem; color:#666;">No forecast data generated yet.</p>
        <p style="color:#999;">Run the forecast generator:</p>
        <code style="background:#f8f9fa; padding:0.5rem 1rem; border-radius:4px;">
          uv run python webapp/_data/generate_forecasts.py
        </code>
      </div>
    `;
  }
}
```

This page shows the Chronos-2 model's predictions overlaid on actual realized values. The best configuration from the search experiment uses **Brent crude oil**, **the policy rate**, and **US CPI** as covariates with a 96-month context window.

## Rolling 1-month-ahead forecasts

Select a target variable to see how the model's 1-month-ahead predictions track reality through time — including the 2008 financial crisis, COVID (2020), and the inflation surge (2022+).

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof selectedVar = Inputs.select(
  ["cpi", "industrial_production", "retail_sales", "unemployment"],
  {label: "Target variable", value: "cpi",
   format: v => v.replace(/_/g, " ").replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase())}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-rolling
//| fig-cap: "Solid line: actual values. Colored dots: model's 1-month-ahead forecasts at each origin."

{
  if (forecasts.length === 0) return html``;

  // Filter h=1 forecasts for selected variable
  const h1 = forecasts
    .filter(d => d.variable === selectedVar && d.h === 1 && d.forecast != null)
    .map(d => ({
      date: new Date(d.target_date),
      forecast: d.forecast,
      origin: new Date(d.origin)
    }));

  // Actual series for selected variable
  const actual = actuals
    .filter(d => d.variable === selectedVar)
    .map(d => ({date: new Date(d.date), value: d.value}));

  // Filter to the forecast range
  const minDate = h1.length > 0 ? d3.min(h1, d => d.date) : new Date("2006-01-01");
  const maxDate = h1.length > 0 ? d3.max(h1, d => d.date) : new Date("2026-01-01");
  const filteredActual = actual.filter(d => d.date >= new Date(minDate.getTime() - 365*24*60*60*1000) && d.date <= maxDate);

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 420,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginBottom: 40,
    x: {type: "utc", label: "Date"},
    y: {label: selectedVar.replace(/_/g, " ").replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase()), grid: true},
    marks: [
      // Era boundaries
      Plot.ruleX([new Date("2006-01-01")], {stroke: "#2ecc71", strokeWidth: 1, strokeDasharray: "4,4"}),
      Plot.ruleX([new Date("2016-01-01")], {stroke: "#e74c3c", strokeWidth: 1, strokeDasharray: "4,4"}),

      // Actual series
      Plot.line(filteredActual, {
        x: "date", y: "value",
        stroke: "#2c3e50", strokeWidth: 1.5
      }),

      // h=1 forecasts
      Plot.dot(h1, {
        x: "date", y: "forecast",
        fill: "#9b59b6", fillOpacity: 0.6, r: 2.5,
        tip: true,
        title: d => `Origin: ${d.origin.toISOString().slice(0,10)}\nForecast: ${d.forecast.toFixed(2)}`
      }),

      // Labels
      Plot.text([{x: new Date("2006-06-01"), text: "Validation"}], {
        x: "x", text: "text", frameAnchor: "top", dy: 15, fontSize: 10, fill: "#2ecc71"
      }),
      Plot.text([{x: new Date("2016-06-01"), text: "Test"}], {
        x: "x", text: "text", frameAnchor: "top", dy: 15, fontSize: 10, fill: "#e74c3c"
      })
    ]
  });
}
```

**Legend:** [Actual values]{style="color:#2c3e50;font-weight:bold"} | [1-month forecast]{style="color:#9b59b6;font-weight:bold"} | [Validation era]{style="color:#2ecc71"} | [Test era]{style="color:#e74c3c"}


## Forecast fan from a specific origin

Select a date to see the model's predictions at all horizons (1, 3, 6, 12 months ahead) fanning out from that point. The dots show where the model predicted the series would be; the line shows where it actually went.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Get available origin dates for the slider
originDates = {
  const dates = [...new Set(forecasts.filter(d => d.variable === selectedVar && d.h === 1).map(d => d.origin))].sort();
  return dates;
}

viewof selectedOriginIdx = Inputs.range(
  [0, Math.max(0, originDates.length - 1)],
  {label: "Origin date", step: 1, value: Math.floor(originDates.length * 0.7),
   format: i => originDates[i] || ""}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
selectedOrigin = originDates[selectedOriginIdx] || originDates[0]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-fan
//| fig-cap: "Fan chart: actual trajectory (dark line) with forecast points at h=1, 3, 6, 12 months."

{
  if (forecasts.length === 0 || !selectedOrigin) return html``;

  const originDate = new Date(selectedOrigin);

  // Get forecasts from this origin
  const fanForecasts = forecasts
    .filter(d => d.variable === selectedVar && d.origin === selectedOrigin && d.forecast != null)
    .map(d => ({
      date: new Date(d.target_date),
      forecast: d.forecast,
      actual: d.actual,
      h: d.h,
      label: `h=${d.h}`
    }));

  // Actual series around this origin (2 years before, 1.5 years after)
  const windowStart = new Date(originDate.getTime() - 2 * 365 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
  const windowEnd = new Date(originDate.getTime() + 1.5 * 365 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
  const windowActual = actuals
    .filter(d => d.variable === selectedVar)
    .map(d => ({date: new Date(d.date), value: d.value}))
    .filter(d => d.date >= windowStart && d.date <= windowEnd);

  // Horizon colors
  const hColors = {"1": "#3498db", "3": "#2ecc71", "6": "#f39c12", "12": "#e74c3c"};

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 380,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginBottom: 40,
    x: {type: "utc", label: "Date", domain: [windowStart, windowEnd]},
    y: {label: selectedVar.replace(/_/g, " ").replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase()), grid: true},
    marks: [
      // Origin line
      Plot.ruleX([originDate], {stroke: "#9b59b6", strokeWidth: 2}),
      Plot.text([{x: originDate, text: `Origin: ${selectedOrigin}`}], {
        x: "x", text: "text", dy: -10, fontSize: 10, fill: "#9b59b6", fontWeight: "bold", frameAnchor: "top"
      }),

      // Actual series
      Plot.line(windowActual, {
        x: "date", y: "value",
        stroke: "#2c3e50", strokeWidth: 2
      }),

      // Forecast points (colored by horizon)
      Plot.dot(fanForecasts, {
        x: "date", y: "forecast",
        fill: d => hColors[String(d.h)] || "#999",
        r: 7, strokeWidth: 1.5, stroke: "white",
        tip: true,
        title: d => `h=${d.h} (${d.date.toISOString().slice(0,10)})\nForecast: ${d.forecast.toFixed(2)}\nActual: ${d.actual != null ? d.actual.toFixed(2) : 'N/A'}`
      }),

      // Actual points at forecast dates (for comparison)
      Plot.dot(fanForecasts.filter(d => d.actual != null), {
        x: "date", y: "actual",
        symbol: "diamond", fill: "#2c3e50", r: 5,
      }),

      // Connect forecast to actual with thin lines
      Plot.link(fanForecasts.filter(d => d.actual != null), {
        x1: "date", y1: "forecast",
        x2: "date", y2: "actual",
        stroke: "#ccc", strokeWidth: 1, strokeDasharray: "2,2"
      })
    ]
  });
}
```

**Legend:** [h=1]{style="color:#3498db;font-weight:bold"} | [h=3]{style="color:#2ecc71;font-weight:bold"} | [h=6]{style="color:#f39c12;font-weight:bold"} | [h=12]{style="color:#e74c3c;font-weight:bold"} | [Actual]{style="color:#2c3e50;font-weight:bold"} (diamonds). Dashed lines connect forecast to actual at each horizon.


## Forecast errors through time

How does the model's 1-month-ahead error evolve? Large spikes indicate periods where the model was surprised — typically during regime changes.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-errors
//| fig-cap: "Forecast error (actual minus forecast) for 1-month-ahead predictions. Positive = model under-predicted."

{
  if (forecasts.length === 0) return html``;

  const errors = forecasts
    .filter(d => d.variable === selectedVar && d.h === 1 && d.forecast != null && d.actual != null)
    .map(d => ({
      date: new Date(d.target_date),
      error: d.actual - d.forecast
    }));

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 250,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginBottom: 40,
    x: {type: "utc", label: "Date"},
    y: {label: "Error (actual - forecast)", grid: true},
    marks: [
      Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#ccc"}),
      Plot.ruleX([new Date("2006-01-01")], {stroke: "#2ecc71", strokeWidth: 1, strokeDasharray: "4,4"}),
      Plot.ruleX([new Date("2016-01-01")], {stroke: "#e74c3c", strokeWidth: 1, strokeDasharray: "4,4"}),
      Plot.dot(errors, {
        x: "date", y: "error",
        fill: d => d.error > 0 ? "#e74c3c" : "#3498db",
        fillOpacity: 0.6, r: 2.5,
        tip: true
      }),
      Plot.line(errors, {
        x: "date", y: "error",
        stroke: "#999", strokeWidth: 0.5, strokeOpacity: 0.5
      })
    ]
  });
}
```
